result.summary: finicity.com’s public developer integration surface is Mastercard Open Finance US (Finicity) HTTP APIs, documented on developer.mastercard.com and authenticated with partner-issued credentials that produce a short-lived access token plus an app key header.
result.description: Finicity, a Mastercard company, provides open finance and account connectivity services in the United States. Its products support use cases such as payments, money management, account aggregation, and lending data access.

partner_creds.label: Partner credentials (Partner ID, Partner Secret, and App Key)
partner_creds.setup: Create or sign in to your Mastercard Developers account at [Mastercard Developers](https://developer.mastercard.com/), create an Open Finance US project, then open the project's **Credentials** section. The Quick Start Guide says you can click **Sandbox** there to view the sandbox `Partner ID`, `Secret`, and `App Key`. These are the credentials required to authenticate to Mastercard Open Finance US / Finicity APIs; the `Partner ID` and `Secret` are exchanged with the Partner Authentication API for a token, and requests also use the `Finicity-App-Key` header.

partner_token.label: Partner access token
partner_token.setup: First obtain your Open Finance US partner credentials from your project in [Mastercard Developers](https://developer.mastercard.com/open-finance-us/documentation/quick-start-guide/): `Partner ID`, `Secret`, and `App Key`. Then call the Partner Authentication API to exchange the `Partner ID` and `Secret` for an access token. The glossary states this token is valid for two hours.
